Section Title: **Flexi Cap Mutual Funds** > ... > **2. High Return Potential**
Content:
These funds stand the chance of earning a high return potential since they invest in large cap companies known for their stable growth, and small cap & mid cap companies are high-growth, high-risk stocks. Further, the fund managers have the flexibility to adjust the fund allocation to get maximum returns.
 ... 
Section Title: **Flexi Cap Mutual Funds** > **What are the returns from Flexi Cap Mutual Funds?**
Content:
For flexi-cap funds whose benchmark is NIFTY 500, average returns have been 13.05% in 3 years, 16.34% in 5 years, and 11.58% in 10 years.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > **What is the difference between Flexi-cap and Multi-cap Mutual Funds?**
Content:
While Flexi-cap and Multi-cap both invest in all three market capitalization. They differ in how their assets are allocated.
With Multi-cap mutual funds, SEBI mandates that the fund manager must allocate 25% each across all market capitalization. So multi-cap mutual funds allocate:
25% in large cap
25% in small cap
25% in mid cap
Unlike Multi-cap, Flexi-cap funds have no fixed allocation. Managers can re-allocate based on market conditions.

results.title: Best Flexi Cap Mutual Funds to invest in India 2026
excerpts:
Section Title: Best Flexi Cap Mutual Funds
Content:
Average Return
16.17%
No of Schemes
122
Flexi-cap mutual funds are open-ended equity funds that do not limit their investment to companies of a predetermined market cap. There are several equity mutual funds that invest in companies of a specific market capitalisation, such as a large-cap, mid-cap, or small-cap.
However, a flexi-cap mutual fund can invest in companies irrespective of their market size. Fund managers of flexi-cap mutual funds have greater flexibility when it comes to making investment decisions. Further, it allows investors to enjoy a diverse portfolio comprising companies of varying market caps from numerous sectors.
 ...

Section Title: Best Flexi Cap Mutual Funds > ... > Fund Performance
Content:
As an investor, it is important to check how the fund has performed in the past. Compare the returns of a flexi cap mutual fund with a benchmark index and its peers before making an investment decision. Investors should also be aware of other factors such as the fund manager’s track record, the fund’s asset allocation trends, and the associated costs & charges.
